title: Adapter assembly for attachment to a bottle
abstract: Various embodiments of the present disclosure describe systems and methods for testing samples (e.g., biological samples, environmental samples, food samples etc.) for microbial contamination. For example, some embodiments describe an adapter assembly with a means to penetrate a septum of a collection vessel and permit gaseous communication between a headspace of the collection vessel and a sensor. In some embodiments, the gases in the headspace of the collection vessel can exit the collection vessel without contaminating the environment outside the system or allowing sample contamination. In some embodiments, the adapter assembly includes a membrane configured to prevent liquid in the collection vessel from contacting the sensor. In some embodiments, the adapter assembly can be used to access media inside the collection vessel for subculturing or aliquotting for another diagnostic process such as molecular diagnostics.
claims:
Claims 1. An adapter assembly comprising: a sleeve configured to engage a collection vessel; a cannula configured to penetrate a septum of the collection vessel; and a sensor assembly interface configured to engage a sensor assembly with a sensor capable of detecting changes in a composition of the gases in a headspace of the collection vessel, wherein the sensor assembly interface is connected to the slee ve and the cannula, wherein the adapter assembly is configured such that when (i) the sleeve is engaged with a collection vessel and (ii) the sensor assembly interface is engaged with a sensor assembly: (a) the sensor assembly interface and the cannula provide a flow pathway for delivering gases from the headspace of the collection vessel to the sensor of the sensor assembly; and (b) the sensor assembly interface defines at least a portion of a chamber for collecting the gases in the flow pathway from the headspace of the collection vessel. 2. The adapter assembly of claim 1 , wherein the adapter assembly is configured to form a closed system when (i) the sleeve is engaged with a collection vessel and (ii) the sensor assembly interface is engaged with a sensor assembly. 3. The adapter assembly of claim 1, wherein the sensor assembly interface includes a membrane configured to: allow gases in the flow pathway to flow toward the sensor: and prevent liquid in the collection vessel from contacting the sensor. 4. The adapter assembly of claim 3, wherein the membrane is interposed between the portion of the sensor assembly interface and the portion of the sensor assembly. 5. The adapter assembly of claim 4, w herein the membrane is a Teflon material embedded with a stainless steel mesh. 6. The adapter assembly of claim 1, wherein the sleeve includes two opposing latches for engaging the collection vessel, and wherein both latches taper inward from a wall of the sleeve in a proximal direction towards a top rim. of the sleeve. 7. The adapter assembly of claim 6, wherein the sleeve includes two pairs of stress relief slots extending longitudinally on opposite sides of the body, and wherein one of the two opposing latches is positioned between one of the two pairs of stress relief slots, and wherein the other opposing latch is position between the other pair of stress relief slots. 8. The adapter assembly of claim 1, wherein the cannula includes a first portion that is substantially cylindrical and a second portion that is tapered to a point at one end, and wherein the first portion includes at least one port. 9. The adapter assembly of claim 8, wherein the cannula comprises two ports located approximately opposite each other on the cannula. 10. The adapter assembly of claim 1 , wherein the sensor assembly interface is an adapter ring having a substantially cylindrical shape. 11. The adapter assembly of claim 10, wherein the adapter ring has a plurality of circumferentially distributed threads for engaging the sensor assembly. 12. The adapter assembly of claim 10, wherein the sleeve includes a substantially cylindrical body, and wherein the adapter ring seals one end of the body of the sleeve, and wherein the cannula is disposed in a central opening of the adapter ring and extends therethrough. 13. The adapter assembly of claim 12 further comprising: a circular spacer disposed on a bottom surface of the adapter ring around the cannula; and an o-ring disposed on a top surface of the adapter ring, wherein the o-ring forms a gas tight seal when the sensor assembly interface is engaged with the sensor assembly. 14. The adapter assembly of claim 1, wherein the sleeve and the sensor assembly interface are monolithically integrated as a single component. 15. The adapter assembly of claim 1, wherein the sleeve and the sensor assembly interface are separate components attached together. 16. The adapter assembly of claim 1 further comprising: 

 the sensor assembly, wherein (i) the sensor assembly is welded to the sensor assembly interface, (ii) attached to the sensor assembly interface with an adhesive, or (iii) the sensor assembly and the sensor assembly interface are monolitliically integrated as a single component. 17. The adapter assembly of claim. 1 further comprising: the sensor assembly; and a septum disposed on a top surface of the sensor assembly such that a needle having a smaller diameter than the cannula can puncture the septum disposed on the top of the sensor assembly and enter the collection vessel through the cannula and the chamber. 18. The adapter assembly of claim 17 further comprising: a first housing comprising the sleeve, the cannula, the sensor assembly interface, and the sensor assembly; and a second housing comprising a needle having a smaller diameter than the cannula, wherein the first housing is loosely fitted inside the second housing. 19. The adapter assembly of claim 18, wherein the second housing is a holder for blood collection. 20. The adapter assembly of claim 18, wherein the needle pierces a seal on the cannula as the first housing is loosely fitted inside the second housing. 21. The adapter assembly of claim 20, wherein the needle has a channel that is in fluid communication with the collection vessel when (i) the sleeve is engaged with a collection vessel, (ii) the sensor assembly interface is engaged with a sensor assembly, and (iii) the first housing is loosely fitted inside the second housing. 22. The adapter assembly of claim 1 further comprising: the sensor assembly, wherein the sensor assembly includes a sensor chamber with the sensor disposed therein; and a septum disposed on a top surface of the sensor assembly such that a needle can puncture the septum and provide access to gases inside the sensor chamber. 23. A method of measuring a composition of gases in a headspace of a collection vessel, the method comprising: 

 engaging a sensor assembly with a sensor assembly interface of an adapter assembly; piercing a septum of a collection vessel with a cannula of the adapter assembly; advancing a sleeve of the adapter assembly along a portion of tlie collection vessel until the adapter assembly is engaged with the collection vessel; and measuring a composition of gases contacting a sensor of the sensor assembly, wherein the sensor is capable of detecting a composition of the gases, and wherein the gases flow from a headspace of tlie collection vessel and towards the sensor through a flow pathway comprising the cannula and a chamber, which is defined, in part, by the sensor assembly interface. 24. The method of claim 23, wherein the steps of securing, piercing, and advancing form a closed system comprising tlie sensor assembly, the adapter assembly, and tlie collection vessel. 25. The method of claim 23 further comprising; installing a membrane between the sensor assembly and the sensor assembly interrace prior to their engagement, wherein the membrane is configured to: allow gases in the flow pathway to flow toward the sensor; and prevent liquid in tlie collection vessel from contacting the sensor. 26. The method of claim 23, wherein the sleeve includes two opposing latches for engaging the collection vessel, and wherein both latches taper inward from a wall of the sleeve in a proximal direction towards a top rim of the sleeve. 27. Tlie method of claim 26, wherein the sleeve includes two pairs of stress relief slots extending longitudinally on opposite sides of the body, and wherein one of the two opposing latches is positioned between one of the two pairs of stress relief slots, and wherein the other opposing latch is position between the other pair of stress relief slots. 28. The method of claim 23, wherein the adapter assembly comprises an adaptor ring having a plurality of circumferentially distributed threads for engaging the sensor assembly. 29. The method of claim 28, wherein the sleeve includes a substantially cylindrical body, and wherein the adapter ring seals one end of the body of the sleeve, and wherein the cannula is disposed in a central opening of the adapter ring and extends therethrough. 30. The method of claim 29 further comprising: 

 installing a circular spacer on a bottom surface of the adapter ring around the cannula; and installing an o-ring on atop surface of the adapter ring, wherein the o-ring forms a gas tight seal when the sensor assembly interface is engaged with the sensor assembly. 31. The method of claim 23, wherein (i) the sensor assembly is welded to the sensor assembly interface, (ii) attached to the sensor assembly interface with an adhesive, or (iii) the sensor assembly and the sensor assembly interface are monoiithically integrated as a single component. 32. The method of claim 23, wherein a septum is disposed on a top surface of the sensor assembly such that a needle having a smaller diameter than the cannula can puncture the septum disposed on the top of the sensor assembly and enter the collection vessel through the cannula and the chamber, and wherein the sleeve, the cannula, the sensor assembly interface, and the sensor assembly are included in a first housing. 33. The method of claim 32 further comprising: inserting a first housing comprising the sleeve, the cannula, the sensor assembly interface, and the sensor assembly into a second housing comprising a needle having a smaller diameter than the cannula, wherein the first housing is loosely fitted inside the second housing. 34. The method of claim 23, wherein the sensor assembly comprises a sensor chamber with the sensor disposed therein: and wherein a septum is disposed on a top surface of the sensor assembly such that a needle can puncture the septum and provide access to gases inside the sensor chamber.
